constraints: Read-only adapter: write_effective=false, native_grant capabilities [read, web]; no file writes, no git mutation, no hook/provider SDK execution (per Brick rules).
Task source forbids editing files, git commit/push, and --all; mandates bounded direct measurements only.

check_profile.py is declared a THIN FACADE / support-evidence-only runner; its --all path carries CHECKER-PROGRESS-GUARD-0 heartbeat+timeout (~L924-1028) and a live-inbox no-write guard (assert_all_profiles_did_not_write_live_fixture_inbox ~L169) — measurement must not trip these.
Several profiles execute live/heavy seams (real provider/onboarding/mcp), so naive full enumeration is itself expensive; measurement must stay per-profile/bounded.

Classification labels (core/dogfood/heavy/historical) are not yet a declared vocabulary in-repo — the later Building needs a labeling scheme decision; this measurement can only surface candidate buckets, not authoritative taxonomy.
missing_scope: No declared core/dogfood/heavy/historical taxonomy or label field exists on profile YAMLs yet; the boundary between buckets is undefined and must be decided (design choice) by the implementation Building, not invented by the measurement.

Per-check/per-profile RUNTIME cost is not measured here (no timing run; --all avoided). Quantifying the reported 5+ min onboard_seam_case / live cases requires bounded timing instrumentation that is itself implementation work to be declared.

Whether any profile_id is truly dead/stale requires cross-referencing every profile_id against all orchestration that selects profiles (CI, operator runners, docs); only the profiles/ directory and core.yaml path_allowlist were inspected here.

Full per-profile kernel_checks membership and the complete KERNEL_DISPATCH id set (KERNEL_CHECK_IDS, ~L921) were sampled via Grep, not exhaustively enumerated; a complete profile→checks→runner table is part of the measurement Building's output.
Relationship of 426594d (named 'mitigation, not root diet' in the task source) to current runner code was not diffed; carried as upstream context only, not verified.
not_proven: brain surface behavior
credential validity
tool or hook execution
runtime or scheduler behavior
quality of returned work

Read tool permission was not granted for absolute paths; all evidence here came from Glob/Grep slices, so exact full-file contents and complete kernel_checks membership per profile are NOT exhaustively verified (environment/runtime constraint, not a defect).
No runtime/timing was measured; the '5+ minutes' onboard_seam_case claim is carried from the task source, not reproduced here.
Whether any profile_id is genuinely dead/stale is not proven — no full cross-repo selector scan was performed.
Whether the mcp_projection.py / mcp_dispatch_wire overlap is true duplication vs intentional layering is not judged (candidate only).
Classification of 426594d as 'mitigation, not root diet' is asserted by the task source and not independently verified against the diff.
checker/profile pass is support evidence only — not source truth, success, quality, or Movement authority.
returned.objective_summary: Bound a READ-ONLY measurement Building that inventories support/checkers/profiles/*.yaml (24 profile YAMLs) and the support/checkers/check_profile.py runner, classifies each profile/check as core-invariant vs dogfood/live-provider/heavy/historical, flags duplicate or over-broad checker mechanisms and dead/stale profile names, and emits a measurement report (exact file paths + bounded commands + evidence refs) that a LATER implementation Building can act on. This plan node only bounds the work; it performs no diet, no edits, no --all run, and asserts no success/quality verdict.
observed_evidence: Glob: 24 YAML files under support/checkers/profiles/ (core.yaml plus 23 named profiles incl. building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening, agent_axis_behavioral, coo_operating_chain, building_operator_driver0, read_side_projection_boundary, mcp_dispatch_wire, tier_a_three_axis_conformance, brick_cli_entrypoint).

check_profile.py header (~L2-5,31,40) declares it a THIN FACADE 'support evidence only' runner; parse_args (~L1320-1331) exposes --profile, --all, --self-test; main raises 'provide --profile, --all, or --self-test' (~L1114).

core.yaml (L4-27) lists ~24 kernel_checks (static boundary checks: axis_vocab_drift, package_path_admission, bricks_spec_completeness, building_map_graph, evidence_spine, etc.) plus path_exists/path_absent/path_allowlist/text_contains rules — these read like CHEAP core-invariant checks.

Heavy/live seams observed: kernel_checks.py:3580 run_onboard_smoke drives REAL run_onboard end-to-end in a tempdir; onboard_seam_case declared in building_operator_driver0.yaml (L85) with host: codex and 'not real-provider proof' limit (L83); read_side_projection_boundary.yaml uses mcp_stdio_smoke (L11); brick_cli_entrypoint.yaml uses brick_cli_entrypoint_smoke (L5); provider_preflight is a KERNEL_DISPATCH runner (~L637).

Grep count of live/seam markers: agent_axis_behavioral.yaml=31, building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml=7, building_operator_driver0.yaml=7 occurrences of live_/case_kind/seam markers; building_skill_preset declares live_linear_n1 / live_dynamic_fan_in_n3 / live_dynamic_full_replay_n3 case_kinds (L1747-1802) — these are the expensive provider-driven cases.

Consolidation precedent observed: read_side_projection_boundary.yaml (L3) is a 'PASS-2 fold' of 5 prior profiles (dashboard_read_side_view, design_toolkit_and_mcp, project_orchestration_ledger, reporter_notification_projection, run_surface_split) — evidence a profile-folding pattern already exists.

Overlap candidate observed: support/connection/mcp_projection.py is referenced by coo_operating_chain.yaml, building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening.yaml, read_side_projection_boundary.yaml, plus a dedicated mcp_dispatch_wire.yaml — multiple profiles touch the same surface (candidate for the later overlap analysis, not a verdict).

--all carries guards: CHECKER-PROGRESS-GUARD-0 heartbeat/timeout (~L924-1028) and assert_all_profiles_did_not_write_live_fixture_inbox (~L169), consistent with the task-source note that progress heartbeat works but heavy cases run long.

Here is the plan Brick return for `task-source:inline-statement` (checker/profile diet measurement). ```json { "objective_summary": "Bound a READ-ONLY measurement Building that inventories support/checkers/profiles/*.yaml (24 profile YAMLs) and the support/checkers/check_profile.py runner, classifies each profile/check as core-invariant vs dogfood/live-provider/heavy/historical, flags duplicate or over-broad checker mechanisms and dead/stale profile names, and emits a measurement report (exact file paths + bounded commands + evidence refs)...
proposed_next_work_boundaries: WORK (measurement): build the complete profile→kernel_checks→runner→proof_limit table for all 24 profiles + KERNEL_DISPATCH, using read-only parsing only.

WORK (classification): tag each profile/check as core-invariant vs live-provider/heavy vs dogfood vs historical, citing declared evidence (e.g. live_* case_kinds, onboard_smoke, mcp_stdio_smoke, provider_preflight).

WORK (overlap/dead-name scan): list candidate duplicate/over-broad mechanisms (e.g. multiple profiles touching support/connection/mcp_projection.py) and any unreferenced profile_id, with path+line refs.
WORK (report emission): write the measurement report artifact with bounded replay commands (single --profile invocations) for a later diet/implementation Building.
These are proposed boundaries only; selection/sequencing and Movement are the Link gate's, not this node's.

scope_boundaries: IN: read-only inventory of support/checkers/profiles/*.yaml (24 files observed via Glob) and check_profile.py runner modes (--profile, --all, --self-test; parse_args ~L1320-1331).
IN: per-profile classification into core-invariant vs dogfood/live-provider/heavy/historical buckets, grounded in declared kernel_checks/case lists and proof_limit text.
IN: identify duplicate/over-broad mechanisms (candidates only) and any dead/stale profile_id values, with exact path + line refs.
IN: produce a measurement report artifact listing evidence refs, file paths, and BOUNDED single-profile commands a later Building can replay.
OUT: editing/renaming/deleting any profile or runner code (that is the later implementation Building).
OUT: running checker/profile --all, git commit/push, or any timing run that touches real provider/onboarding paths.
OUT: choosing the Link Movement or declaring the diet 'done'/'correct'.
